data.title: Oil Major Dives Deep Into Canada Amid Middle East War
data.description: Shell announced a new deal that enhances its presence in Canada while reducing costs and emissions. This move secures the company's access to shale gas and liquid reserves. The oil major's investment comes at a time of conflict in the Middle East.

data.title: Google To Increase Anthropic Investment; OpenAI, Microsoft Shake Up Partnership
data.description: Alphabet intends to boost its investment in Anthropic by an additional $10 billion, with the possibility of increasing this amount to a total of $40 billion. Following this announcement, Google's stocks experienced a slight increase. In other related news, there are changes in partnerships as OpenAI and Microsoft make adjustments, alluding to a shift in the dynamics of the tech investment landscape.

data.title: Litecoin corrects transaction history post exploit, apologizes for social media posts. Attacker made unauthorized transfers, later removed.
data.description: Over the weekend, Litecoin experienced a bug that enabled an attacker to conduct unauthorized transfers of digital assets. The bug resulted in a total of X fraudulent transactions before they were effectively reversed and erased from the system. This security issue raised concerns within the cryptocurrency community regarding the vulnerability of Litecoin's platform.

data.title: Same Day ACH Per Payment Limit to Increase to $10 Million
data.description: In San Diego, the Nacha membership has approved a Rule change increasing the Same Day ACH per payment limit to $10 million. This change, announced at the Smarter Faster Payments 2026 conference, will become effective on September 17, 2027. This marks the third increase in the history of Same Day ACH, following the initial $25,000 maximum which was raised to $100,000 in 2020 and then to $1 million in March 2022. Nacha foresees that the new $10 million cap will enrich the use cases for Same Day ACH transactions.

data.title: Netsmart Showcases Proven Impact of Meaningful AI for Community-Based Care Providers at NatCon26
data.description: Netsmart, a top technology platform company for community-based providers, will be participating in the National Council for Mental Wellbeing 2026 Conference in Denver from April 27-29. With a history of almost 20 years working with the National Council, Netsmart demonstrates its dedication to improving mental health and substance use services. The conference aims to address key issues in the industry and facilitate collaboration among professionals. This event highlights Netsmart's ongoing commitment to enhancing access to quality mental health services.

data.title: Aledade Ranks Among 2026 Top Workplaces for Engineers by Engineering.com
data.description: A national leader in value-based care, Aledade, founded by physicians, has been named one of the Top Workplaces for engineers in 2026 by Engineering.com. Headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland, Aledade stands out as a pioneer in the field, emphasizing quality care and innovative practices. This recognition highlights the company's commitment to creating a positive and rewarding work environment for its employees.

data.title: Boosalis Properties Named Among CoStar's Top Retail Brokers in Washington, D.C. Market
data.description: A commercial real estate firm located in Prince William County, Boosalis Properties, was recently acknowledged by CoStar Group for its outstanding performance in the Washington, D.C. area. The principal broker, George Boosalis, was also recognized for his contributions. This recognition further solidifies the firm's reputation as one of the top retail brokerage firms in the region.

data.title: Virbac : Public release of Virbac Annual Report at 31 December 2025.
data.description: The Virbac Group has publicly released its Annual Report as of December 31, 2025. The company submitted and disclosed its 2025 Annual Report to the French "Autorité des marchés financiers" in the ESEF format. Alongside this regulatory filing, the report has been provided as an attachment. Additional details can be found in the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Foundry Opens New Outpatient Treatment Center in RiNo, Expanding Access to Trauma-Integrated Addiction Care in Downtown Denver
data.description: Denver, on April 27, 2026 – Foundry Outpatient Denver, a division of NRT Behavioral Health, has unveiled the debut of its latest outpatient treatment facility in Denver’s RiNo (River North) Art District. This center is purpose-built to cater to the increasing demand for adaptable and top-tier care services.
